compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Finding

`PlanContext` assembly (`orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached`) swallows child-enumeration and per-child-fetch failures with a bare `except Exception`, silently continuing with an empty/partial `children` list. Because `PlanContext.has_children` is `bool(self.children)`, a total enumeration failure also flips `has_children` to `False`, which makes container-scoped DET checks (confirmed: `det_floor.p5_task_dag`) return a trivial `pass` instead of reporting incompleteness — so a container whose hierarchy failed to load can still reach an unqualified clean PASS. This violates parent epic `6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9` AC #4: "Plan review cannot produce an ordinary clean pass when the ticket hierarchy was loaded incompletely, unless the omitted context is demonstrably irrelevant."

## Evidence

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:173-187` (`_assemble_context_uncached`): `list_tickets(parent=canonical, ...)` wrapped in `try/except Exception` -> `listed = []` on total failure (line 173-178); per-child `show_ticket` wrapped similarly (line 184-187), falling back to the list summary row on partial failure. Neither path records that anything was lost.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py:104-138` (`PlanContext.has_children` property, line 133-138): `return bool(self.children)`. `p5_task_dag` (line 362-368): `if not ctx.has_children: return DetResult("P5", "task-dag", "pass", coverage={"ran": True, "children": 0})` — confirms a wiped `children` list produces a silent P5 pass, not an incompleteness signal. `p8_reviewability`'s container pairing check (~line 598-604) is likewise skipped when `has_children` is `False`.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`: `plan_review_precheck` (def at line 135) calls `orchestrator.assemble_context` at line 140, giving it the REAL `pctx` in scope; its only `finalize_verdict` call is at line 209, **inside `plan_review_precheck`** (not `plan_review_coach`), with `coverage={"det": det_cov, "llm_ran": False}` (line 213). `plan_review_coach` (def at line 503) builds a **new, minimal** `PlanContext(ticket_id=..., ticket_type=..., title="", description="")` at line 540-545 (no `children`/hierarchy data) and calls `finalize_verdict` at line 557, with `coverage` built at line 546-550 from `ctx.inputs.get("det_coverage")` (line 547) — i.e. call site 2 has no live `pctx` and must source everything from the precheck step's dict output.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:503-541` (`finalize_verdict`): computes `verdict` from `coverage.get(...)` flags only (`llm_unavailable` line ~527, `verify_failed` line ~529); it does **not** read any attribute off its `ctx: PlanContext` argument today — confirming `coverage` (not the `ctx` object) is the correct, established plumbing point for a new flag.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` has **three additional** `finalize_verdict` call sites beyond workflow_ops.py's two, none in the original plan:
  - `_recover_plan_review_coach_failure` (def line 298), `coverage` built lines 324-329 from `precheck.get("det_coverage")` (line 325) where `precheck = succeeded.get(STEP_PRECHECK)` (a **dict**, the recorded step output) — same shape as `plan_review_precheck`'s return value.
  - `_recover_plan_review_verify_failure` (def line 342), `coverage` built lines 381-389, identical `precheck.get("det_coverage")` pattern (line 382).
  - `_degraded_plan_review_verdict` (def line 402), `coverage` built lines 420-426 from `det_floor.det_coverage(det_results)` (line 421) where `det_results = det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` (line 412) and `ctx` here **is** the caller's real `PlanContext` (passed straight through from `produce_plan_review_verdict`'s own `ctx` param, lines 145-147/185-187) — so this site, unlike the other two, has direct access to the real `pctx.hierarchy_incomplete` and doesn't need dict-threading.
- `src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_precheck_output.schema.json`: `"additionalProperties": false` (line 17) and `"required": [...]` (line 16) list exactly `run_llm, verdict, canonical_id, ticket_type, det_blocking, det_advisory, det_coverage` — no room for new fields as written. `det_coverage` itself is declared as `{"type": "object", "additionalProperties": true}` (line 14) and IS in `required`.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/lint_refs.py:445-450`: `props = schema.get("properties")`, `literals = frozenset(props.keys())` — an output-contract ref-checker built directly from this schema's `properties`; an unlisted field name used in a `${{ steps.<id>.outputs.<name> }}` ref would lint as UNKNOWN.
- Retry precedent: `_call_with_retry` genuinely exists, but at `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py:113-139` (the **Jira reconciler**, not a generic helper), retrying on `JiraAPIError`/`urllib.error.HTTPError` (429/5xx only, 4xx fails fast) and `TimeoutError`, with `delays=[1,2,4]` seconds — an HTTP-transport-specific predicate that doesn't transfer to a local ticket-store read. `structured.py:294-297` / `runner.py:510-582` implement `OUTPUT_RETRIES=2`, a single bounded reask that feeds the model its own validation error back — also not applicable (LLM-output-validation-specific, not a plain read retry). `src/rebar/_reads.py:53-94`: `show_ticket` (line 53-62) wraps `ticket_reads.ReadError` into `RebarError`, but `list_tickets`'s sole implementation `list_by_query` (line 65-76) calls `ticket_reads.list_states(tracker, query)` with **no try/except at all** — its exceptions propagate unwrapped, not as `RebarError`. Both paths are still caught by `_assemble_context_uncached`'s existing broad `except Exception`, so the planned remediation (catch `Exception`, not `RebarError`) is unaffected by this distinction. No reusable predicate/backoff precedent applies to this call; a bespoke tiny retry is justified, specified concretely below.

## Remediation

1. In `_assemble_context_uncached` (`orchestrator.py:163-211`), wrap the `list_tickets` call (line 174) in a bounded retry: **2 attempts total** (1 retry), catching the same broad `Exception` it catches today, with a **fixed 50ms delay** between attempts. On exhaustion set `pctx.hierarchy_incomplete = True` and `pctx.hierarchy_incomplete_detail = ["enumeration"]`. Add both fields to `PlanContext` (`det_floor.py:104-131`) with defaults `False` / `field(default_factory=list)` — a bare `= []` default on a `@dataclass` list field raises `TypeError` ("mutable default <class 'list'> for field ... is not allowed: use default_factory") at class-definition time (matching the existing `children: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)` pattern at `det_floor.py:114`). Apply the same bounded-retry-then-flag treatment to the per-child `show_ticket` fetch (line 184-187); on a per-child failure, append that child's id (not `"enumeration"`) to `hierarchy_incomplete_detail` and set `hierarchy_incomplete = True`, continuing with the summary row for that child (existing fallback behavior unchanged).
2. Thread `hierarchy_incomplete`/`hierarchy_incomplete_detail` through **all five** `finalize_verdict` call sites via the `coverage` dict (the mechanism `finalize_verdict` already uses for `llm_unavailable`/`verify_failed`), sourced differently per site since only some have the real `pctx`:
   - `workflow_ops.py:135` `plan_review_precheck`, call site line 209: real `pctx` in scope -> `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete"] = pctx.hierarchy_incomplete`, `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"] = pctx.hierarchy_incomplete_detail`. Also add both fields to the `base` dict (line 141-147) returned by this step so they flow to `ctx.inputs` downstream.
   - `workflow_ops.py:503` `plan_review_coach`, call site line 557: no real `pctx` -> `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete"] = ctx.inputs.get("hierarchy_incomplete", False)`, `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"] = ctx.inputs.get("hierarchy_incomplete_detail", [])`, mirroring the existing `ctx.inputs.get("det_coverage")` at line 547. `ctx.inputs` is populated entirely from the step's YAML `with:` block, so this only works once the YAML itself passes the two new fields: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` has exactly four `uses: plan_review_coach` steps (confirmed at lines 205, 219, 285, 299 — one `then`/`else` pair per branch arm, both branch arms identical), each with a `with:` block ending in `ticket_type: ${{ steps.precheck.outputs.ticket_type }}` that must gain two more lines: `hierarchy_incomplete: ${{ steps.precheck.outputs.hierarchy_incomplete }}` and `hierarchy_incomplete_detail: ${{ steps.precheck.outputs.hierarchy_incomplete_detail }}`. Without this YAML edit, `ctx.inputs.get("hierarchy_incomplete", False)` always returns `False` and the coach-path threading is silently defeated.
   - `gate_dispatch.py:298` `_recover_plan_review_coach_failure`, coverage built ~line 324: `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete"] = precheck.get("hierarchy_incomplete", False)`, `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"] = precheck.get("hierarchy_incomplete_detail", [])` (same `precheck` dict already used for `det_coverage`).
   - `gate_dispatch.py:342` `_recover_plan_review_verify_failure`, coverage built ~line 381: identical `precheck.get(...)` pattern.
   - `gate_dispatch.py:402` `_degraded_plan_review_verdict`, coverage built ~line 420: real `ctx` (`PlanContext`) is passed straight through -> `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete"] = getattr(ctx, "hierarchy_incomplete", False)`, `coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"] = getattr(ctx, "hierarchy_incomplete_detail", [])`.
3. In `finalize_verdict` (`orchestrator.py:503-541`), add `elif coverage.get("hierarchy_incomplete"): verdict = "INDETERMINATE"` alongside the existing `llm_unavailable`/`verify_failed` branches (same `elif` chain, checked before falling through to PASS). No relevance-exception carve-out — see item 6 below for why.
4. In `drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop` (`drift_floor.py:190-211`), add the identical `elif cov.get("hierarchy_incomplete"): verdict["verdict"] = "INDETERMINATE"` branch so a dropped BLOCK re-derives INDETERMINATE, not PASS, at all three call sites: `drift_floor.apply_to_verdict` (call at `drift_floor.py:272`), `xcheck.apply_contradiction_drops` (def `xcheck.py:126`, call at `xcheck.py:180`), `xcheck.apply_comment_trail_drops` (def `xcheck.py:258`, call at `xcheck.py:311`).
5. Update `src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_precheck_output.schema.json`: add to `properties` — `"hierarchy_incomplete": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True iff child enumeration or a per-child fetch failed during context assembly."}` and `"hierarchy_incomplete_detail": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "description": "Failed child ids, or [\"enumeration\"] on total list_tickets failure."}`. Do **not** add either to `required` — a missing key reads as falsy/empty everywhere it's consumed (item 2's `.get(..., False)`/`.get(..., [])`), so existing recorded verdicts / older step outputs without the field stay schema-valid. Leave `additionalProperties: false` as-is (both new keys are now declared). Rollback direction: if this schema change is ever reverted, run records already written by the new code (containing `hierarchy_incomplete`/`hierarchy_incomplete_detail`) would fail the reverted schema's closed `additionalProperties: false` validation when read back by `gate_dispatch.py`'s recovery paths (`precheck = succeeded.get(STEP_PRECHECK)`). This is an accepted tradeoff, not a gap to remediate here: schema field additions in this codebase are forward-only (no prior art for a schema-rollback data migration), and reverting an additive schema change without also migrating or discarding the records it wrote is a pre-existing risk of any schema evolution, not one this ticket introduces or must solve.
6. Parent-epic AC #4 reconciliation: parent epic `6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9` AC #4 reads verbatim: *"Plan review cannot produce an ordinary clean pass when the ticket hierarchy was loaded incompletely, unless the omitted context is demonstrably irrelevant."* This leaf intentionally implements the fail-closed half only, with no relevance-exception carve-out, because the exception's own trigger condition — "the criteria that needed the missing context were still decided" — cannot be stated safely here: confirmed via `det_floor.py:104-138`/`362-368` that a failed enumeration collapses `children` to `[]`, which flips `PlanContext.has_children` to `False`, which in turn makes exactly the container-scoped checks that would consume the missing hierarchy (`P5` confirmed; `P8`'s container pairing check similarly gated) return a trivial `pass` rather than running against absent data. So "every criterion was decided despite incomplete context" is never a real state to detect — the container-specific criteria are unconditionally the ones suppressed, meaning any relevance carve-out would be vacuous by construction. Always-INDETERMINATE is therefore the correct, narrower implementation of AC #4 for this context-assembly failure mode, not a deviation from it.
7. Test-case content assertions: add a total-enumeration-failure test asserting `hierarchy_incomplete_detail == ["enumeration"]` exactly (not just that `hierarchy_incomplete` is truthy), and a partial-failure test (one child's `show_ticket` raises) asserting `hierarchy_incomplete_detail` contains that specific child's ticket id.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `PlanContext` gains `hierarchy_incomplete: bool = False` and `hierarchy_incomplete_detail: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)` (`det_floor.py`).
- [ ] `_assemble_context_uncached`'s `list_tickets` call retries once (2 attempts total, 50ms fixed delay, same broad `Exception` predicate as today) before setting `hierarchy_incomplete=True`, `hierarchy_incomplete_detail=["enumeration"]` on total failure.
- [ ] The per-child `show_ticket` fetch gets the same bounded-retry-then-flag treatment; on a per-child failure the child's id is appended to `hierarchy_incomplete_detail` and `hierarchy_incomplete=True` is set, with the existing summary-row fallback unchanged.
- [ ] `plan_review_precheck` (`workflow_ops.py:135`) reads `pctx.hierarchy_incomplete`/`pctx.hierarchy_incomplete_detail` directly (real `pctx` in scope) and threads both into its `base`/`coverage` dicts, used at its own `finalize_verdict` call (line 209).
- [ ] `plan_review_coach` (`workflow_ops.py:503`) reads both fields via `ctx.inputs.get(...)` (mirroring `det_coverage`), NOT via any `pctx` attribute (its `pctx` at line 540 is a minimal reconstruction with no hierarchy data) — no contradiction between AC language and code here.
- [ ] All three `gate_dispatch.py` recovery/degrade paths (`_recover_plan_review_coach_failure`, `_recover_plan_review_verify_failure`, `_degraded_plan_review_verdict`) thread `hierarchy_incomplete`/`hierarchy_incomplete_detail` into the `coverage` dict they build, per Remediation item 2 — five `finalize_verdict` call sites total are covered, not two.
- [ ] `finalize_verdict` (`orchestrator.py`) and `drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop` (`drift_floor.py:190-211`) each gain an `elif coverage.get("hierarchy_incomplete"): verdict = "INDETERMINATE"` branch; the drift_floor branch is exercised through all three call sites (`drift_floor.apply_to_verdict`, `xcheck.apply_contradiction_drops`, `xcheck.apply_comment_trail_drops`).
- [ ] `plan_review_precheck_output.schema.json` declares `hierarchy_incomplete` (boolean) and `hierarchy_incomplete_detail` (string array) in `properties`, neither in `required`; `additionalProperties: false` is preserved.
- [ ] All four `uses: plan_review_coach` steps in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` (both branch arms' `then`/`else`) have their `with:` block extended with `hierarchy_incomplete: ${{ steps.precheck.outputs.hierarchy_incomplete }}` and `hierarchy_incomplete_detail: ${{ steps.precheck.outputs.hierarchy_incomplete_detail }}`, so `ctx.inputs.get(...)` in `plan_review_coach` actually receives the flag instead of always defaulting to `False`.
- [ ] No relevance-exception carve-out is implemented; the ticket body documents why (Remediation item 6), citing parent epic `6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9` AC #4 verbatim and the `has_children`/container-criteria-suppression mechanism that makes the carve-out's trigger condition unstatable.
- [ ] Test coverage includes: (a) total-enumeration-failure -> `hierarchy_incomplete_detail == ["enumeration"]` exactly; (b) partial per-child failure -> `hierarchy_incomplete_detail` contains the specific failed child id; (c) both cases -> verdict is `INDETERMINATE` at at least one of the five `finalize_verdict` sites and survives a drift-floor/xcheck drop without reverting to PASS.

file_impact.reason: Child hierarchy loading and review outcome

compiled_state.title: Prevent clean plan-review passes with incomplete hierarchy context
